channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Ukraine claims North Korean troops were killed as they fought alongside Russian forces
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine’s military intelligence agency claimed Monday that around 30 North Korean troops were killed or wounded in fighting against the Ukrainian army at the weekend in Russia’s Kursk border region.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A Biden proclamation will establish a national monument for the 1st US female Cabinet secretary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden will sign a proclamation Monday establishing a national monument honoring the late FDR-era Labor Secretary Frances Perkins, the first woman appointed to serve in a presidential Cabinet and a driving force behind the New Deal, according to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: LeBron James returns to score 18 in Lakers' win vs Grizzlies after missing 2 games with foot injury
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — LeBron James returned to the Los Angeles Lakers on Sunday night in a 116-10 victory over the Memphis Grizzlies after a two-game break. James is fighting a left foot injury and and was away from the team most of last week because of personal reasons.
item.dc:creator: KYLE GLASER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: France rushes help to Mayotte, where hundreds or even thousands died in Cyclone Chido
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — France was rushing help by ship and military aircraft to its poor overseas territory of Mayotte in the Indian Ocean on Monday after the island was shattered by its worst storm in nearly a century.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Death toll in Gaza Strip from Israel-Hamas war tops 45,000, Palestinians say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The death toll in the Gaza Strip from the 14-month war between Israel and Hamas militants has topped 45,000 people, Palestinian health officials said Monday, with 52 dead arriving at hospitals across the bombed-out strip over the past 24 hours.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump girds for battle with Democrats, Supreme Court over birthright citizenship
item.description: President-elect Trump, Democratic lawmakers and the Supreme Court are on a collision course over restricting birthright citizenship, which Trump has identified as a top priority for his incoming administration.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As schools cut back on bus service, parents turn to rideshare apps
item.description: Reliance on school buses has been waning for years as districts struggle to find drivers and more students attend schools far outside their neighborhoods.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 2 teens killed, 4 injured in shooting at makeshift club in Houston
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Two teens were killed and four were injured — including a 13-year-old girl — in a shooting at a makeshift club in Houston, police said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Takeaways from AP's reporting on a child sex abuse crisis inside a secretive Christian sect
item.description: For decades, a little-known Christian sect has dealt with its abusive ministers and spiritual elders mostly in secret, shunning legal action and urging victims to simply forgive the predators in their midst.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Boone, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 20 Pennsylvania residents charged for multi-million dollar Medicaid fraud
item.description: “The Medicaid program is designed to help vulnerable people in the Commonwealth, not line the pockets of those who deliberately exploit it,” Attorney General Henry said.
item.dc:creator: Bill Shannon, NewsNation

item.title: Remains of missing Wisconsin woman found on private property after years of searching
item.description: Attorney General Josh Kaul expressed sympathy for Ayon’s loved ones, thanking those who worked tirelessly on the case.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Wenz, NewsNation

item.title: US agencies should use advanced technology to identify mysterious drones, Schumer says
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — After weeks of fear and bewilderment about the drones buzzing over parts of New York and New Jersey, elected officials are urging action to identify and stop the mysterious flights. “There’s a lot of us who are pretty frustrated right now,” Rep. Jim Himes, D-Conn.
item.dc:creator: BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS and DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Three arrested for beating bank worker, robbing ATM
item.description: "They appear to purposely break the ATM. Then, they wait for the service person to show up," said Major Jason Allen.
item.dc:creator: Mecca Thompson/KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: Vintage performances by Davante Adams and Aaron Rodgers carry Jets past defenseless Jaguars 32-25
item.description: Davante Adams and Aaron Rodgers gave the New York Jets' next general manager and head coach something to ponder with a glimpse into the past — and potentially the future.
item.dc:creator: MARK LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mahomes throws 2 TD passes, leaves with ankle injury in Chiefs' 21-7 win. X-rays negative on star QB
item.description: Mahomes threw two touchdown passes before limping off with an ankle injury in the fourth quarter, Xavier Worthy ran for a score and Kansas City finally had a relatively easy win, 21-7 over the mistake-prone Cleveland Browns.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Indiana man arrested after asking for a ride and then shooting at the driver
item.description: An Angola man was arrested after asking a woman for a ride, and then allegedly shooting at her in her car when she arrived.
item.dc:creator: Marin Murphy, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Storms across US bring heavy snow, dangerous ice and a tornado in California
item.description:
A tornado near a mall in central California swept up cars, uprooted trees and sent several people to the hospital. In San Francisco, authorities issued the first-ever tornado warning.
Elsewhere, inclement weather plagued areas of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Southampton fires Russell Martin after 5-0 rout by Spurs on a bad day for Premier League managers
item.description: Southampton fired manager Russell Martin late Sunday after the Premier League's last-placed team was thrashed 5-0 by Tottenham.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Miami Dolphins wide receiver Grant DuBose taken off the field on a stretcher after hit to the head
item.description: Grant DuBose, who returned Sunday after a shoulder injury sidelined him for part of the season, was listed as being in stable condition.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: California DMV apologizes for Cybertruck's license plate but family cites misinterpretation
item.description: On Thursday, the license plate in question, which was on a Tesla Cybertruck, was spotlighted in a post to X by StopAntisemitism, a group “dedicated to fighting anti-Semitism.” The family, however, argues it is being misread.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, ABC 4

item.title: California man dies after being mauled by his own dogs: police
item.description: A man was mauled by his three dogs at a neighborhood park Thursday afternoon, according to the San Diego Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Tony Shin, KTLA 5

item.title: Southern California residents report seeing drones in sky amid national concern
item.description: Much of America remains concerned about mysterious drones being sighted in the night sky across the country, and SoCal residents are on high alert too.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Romney: ‘You can’t complain’ if Trump does what he says he would do
item.description: “Donald Trump won. He won overwhelmingly. He said what he was going to do, and that’s what he’s doing,” Romney said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Spotify too repetitive? Here's how to retrain the algorithm
item.description: Over the past couple years, complaints have piled up in online forums from music lovers having trouble discovering new music. Why? No matter what they do, Spotify seems to be serving them the same old songs, again and again.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: Millionaire boomers less likely to share their wealth: Charles Schwab
item.description: Data revealed that baby boomers, born from 1946 to 1964, plan to spend most of their wealth rather than reserve it for future generations.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: VIDEO: San Francisco cafe employee takes down would-be thief
item.description: The video (above) shows a man wearing a black hoodie and mask walking into the cafe and trying to take an item from a customer dining inside.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump gets warm welcome as he watches Army-Navy game alongside key allies, nominees and Daniel Penny
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump huddled with allies and a Republican cause célèbre at Saturday's Army-Navy football game, taking in one of the most storied rivalries in college sports while spotlighting his emerging national security team.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, TARA COPP and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jamie Foxx reportedly injured in altercation at Beverly Hills restaurant
item.description: Foxx, who turned 57 on Friday, was dining with family at Mr. Chow when patrons at a nearby table hurled rude and vulgar insults to the "In Living Color" and "Ray" star, a witness told TMZ.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Storms encase Iowa and eastern Nebraska in ice and generate rare tornado warning in San Francisco
item.description: A major ice storm created treacherous driving conditions across Iowa and eastern Nebraska this weekend and prompted temporary closures of Interstate 80 after numerous cars and trucks slid off the road.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Fox 5

item.title: 6 ways to avoid a financial holiday hangover
item.description: The festivity of December is replaced all too quickly by the due dates of January, when the bills from holiday spending and travel arrive.
item.dc:creator: Kimberly Palmer, KTLA 5

item.title: A look back at the most notable bankruptcies of 2024
item.description: This year saw its fair share of bankruptcies, with household names and century-old companies in the mix.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KRON4

item.title: Obesity rate among US adults fell in 2023 for first time in over a decade: Study
item.description: The obesity rate among U.S. adults fell in 2023 for the first time in over a decade, according to a new study released on Friday. The study published in the journal JAMA Health Forum found that obesity nationwide has dropped from 46 percent in 2022 to 45.6 percent last year.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former governor spots mystery drones in Maryland, blasts feds for lack of transparency
item.description: Former Republican Gov. Larry Hogan of Maryland has said that he personally witnessed "dozens of large drones" flying above his home in Davidsonville, Maryland, on Thursday evening.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court will take up a challenge related to California's tough vehicle emissions standards
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Friday said it would take up a business-backed appeal that could make it easier to challenge federal regulations, acting in a dispute related to California's nation-leading standards for vehicle emissions.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court will hear Catholic charitable group's plea to be free from Wisconsin unemployment tax
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Friday said it would take up a new religious rights case over whether a Catholic charitable organization must pay Wisconsin's employment tax.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Lawmakers demand answers about mysterious drones
item.description: Federal investigators say they've gotten reports about drones flying over military bases and even near President-elect Donald Trump's golf course in New Jersey, but they don't know who's flying them.
item.dc:creator: Vinay Simlot, WGN 9
